title: Issue-body shape per type + universal body rules + checkbox semantics
description:
Formal shape of body-format.yaml — per-issue-type required sections (with checkbox flags and severity), the universal body rules (first-line parent ref, no h1, no file:line, no implementation recipes, no predicted decision IDs), the optional `Integration:` pre-line shape, the checkbox semantics (validated at tick, hard-stop at close, sticky ticks, two kinds: inline + issue-reference), and the sub-task promotion procedure. Consumed by the create-issue and validate-body skills at filing and edit time. Companion per COR-018.

Key constraint: `bodies` keys are kebab-case issue-type ids drawn from issue-types.yaml's `types` namespace.

bodies.description: Per-issue-type body shape. Keys are issue-type ids; values match $defs.entry.

universal_body_rules.description: Rules every issue body must satisfy regardless of type. Each rule is tagged with a severity.

integration_marker.description: Shape and semantics of the optional `Integration: integration/<slug>` pre-line that the agent stamps and removes on owning-issue designation.

checkbox_rules.description: Checkbox semantics: when validation happens (tick time), how closure is gated, which two ticked-states the rule recognises.

sub_task_promotion.description: Mechanics of promoting a markdown sub-task to a standalone Task.

required_sections.description: Sections the body must contain, in any order. Each carries a `## <Name>` heading.

optional_section_recommendations.description: Section headings templates may add beyond the minimum. Not enforced; the floor is `required_sections`. Common entries: `## Why`, `## Out of scope`, `## Related`, `## Dependencies`, `## Approach`, `## Implementation notes`.

heading.description: Exact section heading, including the leading `## ` and a space.

purpose.description: One-line description of what this section captures.

has_checkboxes.description: Whether the section is expected to carry checkboxes that participate in the close-gate per checkbox_rules.

severity.description: Severity applied if the section is missing or empty at filing/edit time.

rule.description: Plain-prose statement of the rule, written so the skill's error message can quote it verbatim.

pattern.description: Regex the marker line must match. `<slug>` is captured.

position.description: Where in the body the line sits — above the parent-ref first line, no blank line between.

agent_managed.description: Whether the agent owns stamping and removal of this line (humans don't edit it directly).

removal_authorisation_severity.description: Severity applied when a human removes the marker without author authorisation (MET-008 scope-change rule).

validation_timing.description: When the actor's claim behind a checkbox is verified. Methodology choice: at tick time, by the actor doing the ticking; never re-validated at close.

close_gate_severity.description: Severity applied when an issue with unticked checkboxes is asked to transition to Done. The methodology's hard stop.

ticked_boxes_sticky.description: Whether un-ticking a previously-ticked box requires an explicit comment explaining the regression.

description.description: When promotion happens — sub-task needs its own PR, owner, discussion, or labels.

new_task_parent_rule.description: Parent of the promoted Task. Per MET-009: the original Task's parent, not the original Task itself.

original_task_body_update.description: What changes in the original Task's body. The plain checkbox becomes a task-list reference (`- [ ] #<N> <text>`); GitHub auto-ticks it when the new Task closes.
